steps.title: Apply the discriminant test for no real solutions
steps.content: For a quadratic $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ to have no real solutions, the discriminant must be negative. Looking at all four choices, $b = \pm 14$, so $b^2 = 196$ for every choice. This means we need $4ac > 196$ to make the discriminant negative.

steps.title: Eliminate choices with negative c
steps.content:
Choices A and C both have negative constant terms ($c = -49$), making $4ac \leq 0$.

Since $b^2 = 196 > 0 \geq 4ac$, the discriminant is positive in both cases—these have real solutions.

Focus on B and D, where $c = +49$. For the discriminant to be negative, we need $4ac > 196$, so $4a(49) > 196$, meaning $a > 1$.

steps.title: Compare the remaining choices
steps.content:
Choice B has $a = 1$, so $4ac = 4(1)(49) = 196$, giving discriminant = $196 - 196 = 0$ (one real solution, not what we want).
Choice D has $a = 5$, so $4ac = 4(5)(49) = 980$, giving discriminant = $196 - 980 = -784 < 0$ (no real solutions). That matches choice D — D is correct.
